diagrams.title: Time Complexity Growth Rate Chart
diagrams.description: A line graph comparing the growth rates of common time complexity functions: O(1), O(log n), O(n), O(n log n), and O(n²). The x-axis represents input size (n) and y-axis represents operations. This helps visualize why certain algorithms become inefficient as input grows.
elements: Multiple colored lines representing different complexity functions
diagrams.purpose: Demonstrates the dramatic difference in growth rates between constant, logarithmic, linear, and exponential time complexities

diagrams.title: Data Structures Time Complexity Comparison
diagrams.description: A structured table/chart showing time complexities (Best, Average, Worst case) for fundamental operations (Access, Search, Insertion, Deletion) across common data structures like Arrays, Linked Lists, Stack, Queue, Binary Search Tree, and Hash Table.
elements: Table header row with operation types
Data structure names in first column
Colored cells indicating complexity level (green for O(1), yellow for O(log n), orange for O(n), red for O(n²))
Worst case notation for each operation
diagrams.purpose: Provides a quick reference for comparing time complexities across different data structures to make informed algorithm design decisions

diagrams.title: Search Algorithm Complexity Visualization
diagrams.description: A visual comparison showing Linear Search versus Binary Search on a sorted array. Demonstrates how Linear Search checks each element sequentially while Binary Search divides the search space in half each step, resulting in O(n) vs O(log n) complexity.
elements: Arrows showing Linear Search stepping through each element
Highlighting showing Binary Search elimination of halves
Step counter showing number of comparisons for each method
Target element highlighted in both searches
diagrams.purpose: Illustrates the practical difference between O(n) and O(log n) complexity through a concrete example, showing why algorithm choice matters
